results.title: Langfuse - $3M Raised - Reviews & Alternatives | StartupHub.ai
results.content: StartupHub.ai — AI Ecosystem Hub. # Langfuse. Open-source LLM engineering platform for debugging, monitoring, and improving production-grade LLM applications. Langfuse is an open-source LLM engineering platform designed to help teams build and improve production-grade LLM applications faster. It addresses key challenges such as debugging complex workflows, monitoring costs and latencies, and assessing output quality, providing comprehensive tools for the entire LLM application lifecycle. ### How much funding has Langfuse raised? Langfuse has raised a total of $3M in funding. ### Where is Langfuse headquartered? Langfuse is headquartered in Berlin, Germany. ### What industry does Langfuse operate in? ### What is Langfuse's revenue? StartupHub estimates Langfuse's annual revenue at $3.4M (range $2.8M to $4.3M, 62% confidence), based on comparable companies in the sector. ### What is Langfuse's valuation? StartupHub estimates Langfuse's effective valuation at $9.6M (range $6.3M to $15.0M, 10% confidence), based on comparable companies in the sector.

results.title: How did we get here? - Langfuse
results.content: With an initial product and customers, we got accepted into YC W23 in November 2022. While our customers were excited about Finto (the billing product), we realized that we were not excited about building the *nth* generation of product in an established (small) market. We built an initial version and had our first customers in the YC batch. We decided that we wanted Langfuse to be open source (more on this here) and published the repo. It quickly became clear that Langfuse *works*, our customers loved the initial product, and we were excited to build it. Over the New Year, we decided to branch out into Prompt Management as (1) many early customers asked for it, and (2) we saw a lot of benefit in a prompt management product that is tightly integrated with the tracing/observability features. In our first Launch Week, we reintroduced Langfuse as the AI Engineering Platform and launched essential features to cover the workflow:.

results.title: Langfuse raises $4M - Langfuse
results.content: **With Langfuse, we are building open source observability and product analytics for LLM-based applications.** Our goal is to enable product and engineering teams to harness the power of Generative AI with the most useful and open suite of devtools focused on visibility and insights. When we first joined Y Combinator for the winter batch in early 2023, we iterated quickly on a number of LLM-native applications ranging from LLM-powered web-scraping — for research and go-to-market use cases — to generating engaging educational content on virtually any topic. As engineers, we initially worked on a test framework to increase the confidence in application outputs while overcoming the problems when applying traditional CI frameworks – which are management of test set, latency and cost of running model-based evaluations in CI, and finding good evaluations in the first place. > “We were one of the first customers of Langfuse and it has really made our lives easier by meeting all our LLM analytics needs, allowing us to focus on building our product with great visibility on how it's used in production and how to best improve it.
